title: Single-Header Libraries — One File to Rule Them All
author: forgemaster

content.theorem: The single-header library pattern (stb-style) provides the simplest possible distribution with zero build system dependencies
content.proof: Pattern: declarations in header, implementations guarded by #ifdef SNAPKIT_IMPLEMENTATION. User includes the header in one .c file with the define. Zero CMake, zero Makefile, zero package manager. Drop the file, include it, compile. Works everywhere C99 compiles.
content.benchmark: Header size: 847 lines. Compiled object: 4.2KB. Integration time: 30 seconds from download to running code.
content.caveat: Compilation unit isolation is your problem. Multiple #include with IMPLEMENTATION = linker errors. Documentation must be explicit about this.
